headline_findings: FRAMEWORK MISIDENTIFIED IN PRIOR ITER: ChatGLM is Vue 3 + Vuex 4 (NOT React). All iter7/8/10 'React state machine' analysis was wrong.
Framework markers: window.__VUE_INSTANCE_SETTERS__, document.getElementById('app').__vue_app__, app.config.globalProperties.$store. DOM uses data-v-* scoped attrs.
Chat-bubble state lives in Vuex Conversation.dataList[i].answerArray[j] (NOT Detail.dataList — Detail.dataList is empty in this view).

answerArray is the engine-side state machine: a sequence of phases (think -> tool_calls -> text). Each phase is ONE entry with content_type/answer_type ∈ {'think','tool_calls','text'} and content_status ∈ {'init','finish'}.

PARTIAL_THINK_ONLY ROOT CAUSE = ENGINE-SIDE: In stuck case, answerArray contained ONLY 1 entry (content_type='think', content_status='init', text=''). The engine emitted the think-init event then the SSE stream was cut off (NEVER emitted think-finish, NEVER emitted text-init/finish).

Concomitant evidence of engine failure: HTTP 400 on /chatglm/mainchat-api/conversation/title (downstream — title API needs completed answer text); WebSocket 'Invalid frame header' on wss://post-cn-1f64bya8e02.mqtt.aliyuncs.com/mqtt (Aliyun MQTT, possibly the SSE transport).

iter9 drop-think strategy WORKED visibly because it bypassed the engine entirely (synthetic text bubble from frontend) — that confirms the Vue render correctly mounts text bubbles given a text-typed entry, but at the cost of NO APF protection (full PII leak).

answer_array_in_success_case_3_phases._role: engine internal COT chain-of-thought (separate PII leak path if 'expand thinking' clicked)
final visible text bubble — full PII leak: '1990년 10월 10일에 태어났음을 알 수 있습니다... 1: 1900년대 출생 남성...'

notes.engine_event_format_inferred: SSE phases keyed by `content_type`/`answer_type` enum (think|tool_calls|text). Each phase: init -> chunks -> finish. New entry pushed to answerArray when a NEW phase begins. The SAME message id ('id') across all phases of one answer; each phase has its OWN unique 'logic_id' (UUID).
notes.transition_condition_for_text_bubble_ren: Vue render iterates message.answerArray and renders each entry's bubble component by content_type. Text bubble appears when an entry with content_type='text' AND text non-empty is added. The frontend has NO recovery logic — it is purely reactive to the engine SSE stream.
hypothesized_fix_for_think_to_text_trans: OPTION A (RECOMMENDED — PRIMARY APF STRATEGY): Intercept at SSE/MQTT layer. APF should hook the SSE stream from /chatglm/mainchat-api/conversation/* (and/or wss://post-cn-1f64bya8e02.mqtt.aliyuncs.com/mqtt MQTT WebSocket — pending confirmation that's the streaming channel). Inject a SYNTHETIC text-phase event with the warning content AFTER the engine emits 'think init' but BEFORE the engine emits actual text chunks. This bypasses the engine think→text transition cleanly without depending on engine cooperation.

OPTION B (VUEX MUTATION HOOK): Wrap store.commit on Conversation module. Detect when an answerArray entry is being added with content_type='text' AND text matches PII patterns. Replace .text with warning. Also detect the stuck case (single entry stuck at think_init for >N seconds) and force-push a synthetic text entry with warning. Pure frontend; no engine dependency.

OPTION C (DOM OBSERVER + INJECTION): Monitor for the .advance-thinking-skip element appearing (think bubble). Wait N seconds. If no .text-bubble appears, inject one via direct DOM manipulation. Less clean but most robust against future Vue refactors.

Note: iter9 'drop-think' strategy is OPTION C without the wait-and-check — it ALWAYS injects text bubble, hence the PII leak. The fix to iter9 is to either (a) inject AFTER detecting engine-side think emission (so we know engine triggered) AND replace the synthetic content with warning text (so engine output never reaches DOM), OR (b) move to OPTION A entirely.
notes.anti_debugger_obstacle_for_future_iter: ChatGLM ships an aggressive setInterval-driven `debugger;` trap that continually re-injects via dynamically-created anonymous functions. Standard 'Disable breakpoints' insufficient. For future SSE capture iterations, recommend Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) over WebSocket from a Python script (playwright/puppeteer) attached BEFORE page load, OR use mitmproxy to intercept TLS-decrypted /chatglm/mainchat-api/* responses externally.

files_saved.react_state_think.json: Full Vue/Vuex state snapshot — both stuck and success cases with complete schema documentation
files_saved.sse_frames.json: Reconstructed event log inferred from observed Vuex state mutations (live SSE capture blocked by anti-debugger; see file's _meta.capture_status)
files_saved.console_log.txt: Full console errors + JS REPL probes + Vuex module enumeration + answerArray field-by-field dump

notes.no_har_file_attached: HAR export not generated this run because (a) Network panel was opened AFTER the original conversation's stream completed, and (b) the second-pass attempt (fresh navigation) was blocked by the anti-debugger trap. HAR is gitignored anyway.
